text:
*[Bug 19089] New: Allow passing of a compression argument to saveRDS*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19089>

            Bug ID: 19089
           Summary: Allow passing of a compression argument to saveRDS
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.6.x
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Linux
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Wishlist
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:tim.taylor@hiddenelephants.co.uk|tim.taylor@hiddenelephants.co.uk>

When `saveRDS()` is called and you are not using a connection, the compression
value remains at the default for the underlying connection (i.e. 6 for
`gzfile`, 9 for `bzfile`, 6 for `xzfile` and 9 for `zstdfile`). It would be
convenient to be able to pass these values directly to `saveRDS()` without
setting up a connection directly. 

Hugo Gruson kindly pointed out to me that there was an off-list discussion of
this topic prior/during one of the previous R dev days (see
<https://github.com/r-devel/r-dev-day/issues/89>). Here there was also a similar
(but more involved due to touching C code) request for memCompress to accept
different levels of compression as an argument.

Note that I've moved this here after allowing for discussions in a [post to the
R-devel mailing
list](<https://stat.ethz.ch/pipermail/r-devel/2026-May/084580.html>). Hopefully
this keeps things a little more discoverable longer term.

text:
*[Bug 19084] Wishlist: as.matrix.POSIXlt not to produce mode "character"*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19084>

--- Comment #12 from Suharto Anggono (<mailto:suharto_anggono@yahoo.com|suharto_anggono@yahoo.com>) ---
With r90157, the example block in DateTimeClasses.Rd containing 'as.matrix'
becomes

## look at *internal* representation of "POSIXlt" :
leapS <- as.POSIXlt(.leap.seconds)
names(unclass(leapS)) ; is.list(leapS)
## str() on inner structure needs unclass(.):
utils::str(unclass(leapS), vec.len = 7)
## show all components
data.frame(unclass(leapS))
## Omitting "tzone" attrib, numeric matrix w/ 10 columns (27 x 10, currently) :
(leapm <- as.matrix(leapS))
stopifnot(identical(ncol(leapm), 10L), is.numeric(leapm)) # was character in R
<= 4.6.0


With the previous comment saying
show all components
, I think saying
Omitting "zone" component
is more logical than
Omitting "tzone" attrib
